exploration_log.aha_moment: The Delimiter concept just requires a Find(text, pos) method returning a string_view of the delimiter match. For ByPredicate, scan from pos and return a 1-character string_view at the first matching position; return empty at end if no match.
exploration_log.hindsight: Returning `text.substr(text.size(), 0)` for no-match is the correct convention as it's an empty view at the end of the text, which StrSplit uses as a sentinel.

key_decisions.reasoning: Other delimiter classes return a view into the original text (not a default-constructed empty view); this is important for StrSplit to correctly detect end-of-input

search_sequence.action: Read str_split.h for existing delimiter class structures
search_sequence.reasoning: Need to understand the Delimiter concept (Find signature, return value convention)
search_sequence.result: Find(text, pos) returns string_view of the delimiter; empty string_view at end means no match found

search_sequence.action: Read ByChar and ByAnyChar Find implementations
search_sequence.reasoning: Need to understand the exact return-value convention for 'not found' case
search_sequence.result: Return a view pointing past the end of text with 0 size when delimiter not found

queries.query_text: Add ByPredicate<Callable> delimiter template to absl/strings/str_split.h with Find method scanning for first character satisfying predicate

solve_notes: Added `ByPredicate<Callable>` template class to `str_split.h`. The `Find` method scans from `pos` and returns a 1-character `string_view` at the first character satisfying the predicate, or an empty view at the end of the text if no match is found (following the same convention as other delimiter classes).
task_text:
The `StrSplit()` function in `strings/str_split.h` provides delimiters
`ByChar`, `ByAnyChar`, `ByString`, `ByAsciiWhitespace`, and `ByLength`,
but has no predicate-based delimiter. Add `ByPredicate<Callable>`
that splits on any character satisfying a callable predicate
`bool(char)`, for example `StrSplit(s, ByPredicate(absl::ascii_ispunct))`
to split on punctuation. Implement the `Find(absl::string_view text,
size_t pos)` method in `str_split.h` that scans for the first character
satisfying the predicate and returns the matching single-character
`absl::string_view`.

thrash_preventing_defs.name: ByChar
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: the Delimiter concept (Find signature, return-value convention) must be understood from an existing delimiter before implementing ByPredicate

thrash_preventing_defs.name: Find
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: the 'no match' return convention (text.substr(text.size(), 0)) must be checked from an existing Find implementation

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set covers only the new class. An AI agent would additionally look at an existing delimiter class (ByChar) and its Find method signature/return conventions to understand the Delimiter concept before implementing ByPredicate. Without this, the agent might return absl::string_view() for no-match instead of text.substr(text.size(), 0), causing incorrect behavior.
